Title: OsGLP3-7 positively regulates rice immune response by activating hydrogen peroxide, jasmonic acid, and phytoalexin metabolic pathways

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: hydrogen peroxide; jasmonic acid; diterpenoid phytoalexins (momilactone A/B, oryzalexin C, phytocassane A/B/C)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: reactive oxygen species; oxylipin phytohormone; diterpenoid phytoalexin
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defence against rice blast, panicle blast, and bacterial blight pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates accumulation of H2O2, jasmonic acid, and phytoalexins, thereby enhancing rice disease resistance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves; also detected in young shoot, root, stem node, and panicle; induced by pathogen infection, jasmonic acid, salicylic acid, and H2O2; repressed by ABA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaves; also detected in young shoot, root, stem node, and panicle; induced by pathogen infection, jasmonic acid, salicylic acid, and H2O2; repressed by ABA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae infection; Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae infection; jasmonic acid treatment; methyl jasmonate treatment; salicylic acid treatment; ABA treatment; H2O2 treatment
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: LOX2; LOX9; AOS2; AOS3; MAS; KSL4; CYP99A2; CYP76M6; CYP76M7; CYP76M8; KSL7; KSL10; KO4; CYP71Z7
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in rice; RNAi knockdown in rice; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; recombinant protein SOD enzyme assay; endogenous H2O2 measurement; LC-MS/MS quantification of jasmonic acid and phytoalexins; disease resistance assays after pathogen inoculation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGLP3-7 overexpression enhanced resistance to leaf blast, panicle blast, and bacterial blight, while RNAi silencing compromised resistance. 2. OsGLP3-7 protein has superoxide dismutase activity and promotes H2O2 accumulation during pathogen infection. 3. OsGLP3-7 activates expression of JA biosynthetic genes and increases jasmonic acid accumulation. 4. OsGLP3-7 induces phytoalexin biosynthetic genes and increases accumulation of momilactones, oryzalexin C, and phytocassanes. 5. OsGLP3-7-dependent phytoalexin accumulation is at least partly attributable to elevated JA levels.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive, for subcellular localization); native OsGLP3-7 promoter (for GUS analysis); PHQSN overexpression vector; pRNAi-Ubi (constitutive RNAi)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering broad-spectrum disease resistance in rice, potentially through transgenic overexpression or editing strategies that elevate OsGLP3-7 activity without visible growth penalty.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant morphological difference was observed in overexpression or RNAi plants relative to controls under the conditions tested.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsGLP3-7-mediated disease resistance was JA-dependent but not SA-dependent. The paper suggests the temporal order during blast infection may be JA, then phytoalexins, then H2O2. OsGLP3-7 was reported in the abstract as cytoplasmic, while subcellular localization figure text states cytoplasmic localization compared with GFP control present in nucleus and cytoplasm.
